segments.text: Men, brethren, and fathers, hear ye my defence which I make now unto you.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: When they heard that he spake in the Hebrew tongue to them, they kept the more silence:

I am verily a man which am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, yet brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, and taught according to the perfect manner of the law of the fathers, and was zealous toward God, as ye all are this day.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And I persecuted this way unto the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.

As also the high priest doth bear me witness, and all the estate of the elders: from whom also I received letters unto the brethren, and went to Damascus, to bring them which were there bound unto Jerusalem, for to be punished.
And it came to pass, that, as I made my journey, and was come nigh unto Damascus about noon, suddenly there shone from heaven a great light round about me.
And I fell unto the ground, and heard a voice saying unto me,
Who art thou, Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: He said unto me,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They that were with me saw indeed the light, and were afraid; but they heard not the voice of him that spake to me.
What shall I do, Lord?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: The Lord said unto me,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: When I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of them that were with me, I came into Damascus.
And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good report of all the Jews which dwelt there,
Brother Saul, receive thy sight.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: And the same hour I looked up upon him.
The God of our fathers hath chosen thee, that thou shouldest know his will, and see that Just One, and shouldest hear the voice of his mouth.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: For thou shalt be his witness unto all men of what thou hast seen and heard.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: And now why tarriest thou? arise, and be baptized, and wash away thy sins, calling on the name of the Lord.
segments.speaker: Ananias

segments.text: And it came to pass, that, when I was come again to Jerusalem, even while I prayed in the temple, I was in a trance;
And saw him saying unto me,
Lord, they know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue them that believed on thee:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And when the blood of thy martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting unto his death, and kept the raiment of them that slew him.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And he said unto me,
Away with such a fellow from the earth: for it is not fit that he should live.
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: And as they cried out, and cast off their clothes, and threw dust into the air,
The chief captain commanded him to be brought into the castle, and bade that he should be examined by scourging; that he might know wherefore they cried so against him.
Is it lawful for you to scourge a man that is a Roman, and uncondemned?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Take heed what thou doest: for this man is a Roman.
segments.speaker: Centurion

segments.text: Tell me, art thou a Roman?
segments.speaker: Chief Captain

segments.text: Yea.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: With a great sum obtained I this freedom.
segments.speaker: Chief Captain

segments.text: But I was free born.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Then straightway they departed from him which should have examined him: and the chief captain also was afraid, after he knew that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.

On the morrow, because he would have known the certainty wherefore he was accused of the Jews, he loosed him from his bands, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down, and set him before them.
